note: Known RTK output forms and how frozen qodec/VG should treat each when composed AFTER RTK. recommended_qodec_policy is advisory for the observational baseline only; the first comparison still runs unmodified frozen VG. No form here authorises changing production VG.

summary.risk: Summary already dropped detail; VG can only fold what remains — savings are small and losses are RTK's, not VG's.

grouped-list.risk: Grouping reorders lines; ordering questions must be answered from RAW, not from RTK grouped output.

failure-list.risk: Non-failure context is discarded; negative-evidence questions can be corrupted.

diagnostic-group.risk: Executable hints (suggested fixes) must survive; a future VG-RTK-v1 should protect them.

deduplicated-log.risk: RTK already removed the repetition VG folds losslessly; incremental VG gain is near zero (measure, do not assume).

tree.risk: Deep trees may be truncated; full-path locator questions can lose leaves.

truncated-list-with-recovery.risk: The recovery hint ([full output: …] / [see remaining: …]) is the ONLY path back to raw; it must never be folded away.

passthrough.risk: RTK returned input unchanged; this is the cleanest case for measuring VG's own contribution.

empty.risk: Empty result; VG must passthrough with no negative savings.

parse-fallback.risk: RTK failed to parse and returned raw; VG operates on true raw — the fair lossless comparison point.
